  • 两河 - The two rivers, referring to the Yellow River and the Yangtze River, significant geographical and cultural regions in Chinese history.
  • 郭老仆 - Old Gu, the name of the old servant, who becomes a central figure in the story.
  • 张山来 - Zhang Shanlai, a commentator providing an additional perspective on the text.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天啟朝 - Refers to the Tianqi era (1621-1627) of the Ming dynasty.
  • 逆珰魏忠賢 - "逆珰" means "rebellious eunuch," and "魏忠賢" is the name of the notorious eunuch Heshen (Wu Zhongxian), who wielded significant power during the Tianqi era.
  • 扇虐 - Means "to fan violence" or "to incite cruelty."
  • 諸卿大夫 - Refers to high-ranking officials and nobles.
  • 以忠直被刑戮 - Means "were punished for their loyalty and integrity."
  • 怨愤彻闾里 - Means "grievances and anger spread throughout the villages."
  • 匹夫匹妇 - Refers to common people, both men and women.
  • 发竖心伤 - Describes a state of intense anger and distress.
  • 公然发愤 - Means "to openly express anger."
  • 抗中贵、殴缇骑 - Means "to resist high-ranking eunuchs and attack their guards."
  • 不恤其身家之殒 - Means "without regard for the loss of their own lives and families."
  • 唯义之殉 - Means "to sacrifice oneself for justice."
  • 若苏民之于吏部周公顺昌者也 - Means "like Su Min who acted on behalf of the Ministry of Personnel Zhou Gong Shunchang."
  • 尝读《颂天胪笔》 - Means "I once read 'Song Tian Lu Bi' (a book praising heaven and recording events)."
  • 询之吴父老 - Means "I asked the elders of Wu (a region)."
  • 未尝不击节慨慕之云 - Means "I always admired and respected them."

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 诸卿大夫 - High-ranking officials and nobles who were often targeted by Heshen.
  • 苏民 - A commoner who took a stand against Heshen.
  • 吏部周公顺昌 - Zhou Shunchang, a high-ranking official in the Ministry of Personnel who was persecuted by Heshen.
  • 天启六年 - 1626, a significant year during the Tianqi era.
  • 织造中使李实 - A eunuch named Li Shi who was involved in the persecution of scholars.
  • 讲学聚徒 - Refers to the practice of teaching and gathering students, which was often seen as a threat by Heshen.
  • 都御史高公攀龙、御史周公宗建、谕德缪公昌期、御史黄公尊素、李公应升 - High-ranking officials who were persecuted by Heshen.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 移時抵西署 - A phrase indicating that after a period of time, they arrived at the Western Office (西署). '移時' means 'after a while' or 'after some time.'
  • 署設帏幕儀仗 - 'The office was set up with curtains and ceremonial banners.' This is a formal description of the setting, indicating a display of authority.
  • 氣張甚 - 'His [Yinglong’s] spirit was greatly inflated' or 'he was very arrogant' – a metaphor for being haughty or overbearing.
  • 最下陳锒铛钮镣諸具 - 'At the lowest rank, they displayed chains, manacles, and other restraints.' This is a description of the instruments of arrest or punishment.
  • 眾目屬哽咽 - 'The crowd looked on in choked silence.' This conveys the emotional reaction of the people.
  • 盍請釋之以慰民乎 - 'Why not request his release to comfort the people?' '盍' is a rhetorical particle meaning 'why not?'
  • 奈聖怒何 - 'What can be done about the emperor’s anger?' A rhetorical question expressing helplessness.
  • 今日之事，實東廠矫诏 - 'Today’s matter is indeed a forged edict from the Eastern Factory (East Depot).' '矫诏' means 'to forge an imperial decree.'
  • 吏部無辜，徒以口舌賈禍 - 'The Ministry of Personnel is innocent; they have only incurred trouble through words.' '賈禍' means 'to bring calamity' or 'to invite disaster.'
  • 明公剀切上陳，幸而得請 - 'If you, Sir, present a sincere and urgent petition, and if you are successful, then the Ministry of Personnel will be saved.' '剀切' means 'sincere, earnest, or forceful.'
  • 直道猶存天壤 - 'The righteous path still exists in heaven and earth.' A metaphor for moral integrity enduring despite adversity.
  • 一鹭周张无以对 - 'Yi Lu was at a loss for words.' '周张' may be a description of being flustered or unable to respond.
  • 缇骑以目相视 - 'The imperial guards looked at each other.' A gesture of uncertainty or doubt.
  • 讶一鹭不以法绳之 - 'They were surprised that Yi Lu did not punish them according to law.' '以法绳之' means 'to punish according to law.'
  • 攘臂直前，訴且泣曰 - 'They raised their arms and stepped forward, pleading and weeping, saying...' '攘臂' is a vivid expression meaning to raise one’s arms in anger or urgency.
  • 必得請乃已 - 'We will not stop until we get a request [for his release].' '乃已' means 'only then will it stop.'
  • 念如故阊门鬻衣人，扬故牙侩 - 'Nianru was once a clothes seller at the Changan Gate; Yang was once a broker.' '鬻衣人' is a clothes seller, and '牙侩' is a broker or intermediary.
  • 皆不習吏部，並不習佩韋者也 - 'None of them were familiar with the Ministry of Personnel, nor were they familiar with Pei Wei.' '不習' means 'not familiar with' or 'not acquainted with.'
  • 蒲伏久之，麾之不肯起 - 'They knelt for a long time, but when ordered to rise, they refused to stand.' '蒲伏' is a term for kneeling or prostration, and '麾之' is a gesture to command someone to rise.
  • 缇骑怒叱之 - 'The imperial guards angrily scolded them.' '叱之' is to scold or rebuke.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西署 - Likely refers to a government office or detention center in the Western area of the capital, possibly part of the imperial administration.
  • 缇骑 - 'Red-cloaked riders,' a term for the imperial guards or eunuch-led enforcers of the Ming or Qing dynasties.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel, one of the six major departments of the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 东厂 - The 'Eastern Factory,' one of the secret police agencies of the Ming dynasty, known for its power and corruption.
  • 矫诏 - 'To forge an imperial decree,' a serious crime in imperial China.
  • 口舌贾祸 - 'To invite disaster through speech or argument,' a common expression in classical Chinese literature.
  • 阊门 - A gate in the city of Suzhou, or a reference to a specific location in a city.
  • 鬻衣人 - A clothes seller, a humble occupation.
  • 牙侩 - A broker or intermediary, often associated with marketplaces or trade.
  • 蒲伏 - To kneel or prostrate oneself, a gesture of submission or reverence.

Proper Nouns

  • 一鹭 - A proper name, likely a high-ranking official or minister.
  • 徐吉 - A proper name, the Circuit Inspector (巡按御史).
  • 佩韋 - 'Pei Wei' is likely a proper name, possibly a high-ranking official or a scholar.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 则马杰也 - Indicates that the person who cursed is identified as Ma Jie.
  • 遂手锒铛，掷阶砉然 - Describes the guards grabbing the cursers and throwing them down the stairs with a loud noise.
  • 乃大哗 - Indicates a loud commotion or uproar.
  • 至是跃出直前夺械 - Zhou Wen Yuan jumps out and tries to take the weapons from the guards.
  • 缇骑笞之，伤其额 - The guards beat him, injuring his forehead.
  • 文元愤，众亦俱愤，遂起击之炳 - Zhou Wen Yuan and the others become enraged and attack someone named Zhi Bing.
  • 之炳跳，众群拥而登，栏楣俱折，脱屐掷堂上，若矢石落 - Zhi Bing jumps away, and the crowd follows, breaking the railings and throwing shoes onto the stage like arrows and stones.
  • 自缇骑出京师，久骄横，所至凌轹 - Describes the guards' long-standing arrogance and tyranny.
  • 郡邑长唯唯俟命 - Local officials are depicted as meekly waiting for orders.
  • 苏民之激，愕出不意，皆踉跄走 - The people of Suzhou are described as being shocked and running away.
  • 一匿署阁，缘桷，桷动，惊而堕，念如格杀之 - One person hides in an office, climbs on a beam, and falls, causing panic.
  • 一踰垣仆淖中，蹴以屐，脑裂而毙 - Another person jumps over a wall and falls into a muddy area, where he is kicked and dies.
  • 其匿厕中、翳荆棘者，俱搜得杀之 - Those hiding in toilets or among thorns are found and killed.
  • 一鹭、吉皆走匿 - Some people named Lu and Ji flee and hide.
  • 王节等知事败，而当众气方张之时，即欲前谕止不可得 - Wang Jie and others realize the situation is lost and try to stop the crowd, but it's too late.
  • 诸父老练事者，亦旋悔，稍稍散 - Experienced elders regret the situation and slowly disperse.
  • 是日也，缇骑之逮御史黄公尊素者，适舟次胥江，掠于郛，执市人挞之 - On the same day, the guards capture a magistrate named Huang Zunsu, who is traveling by boat, and beat a market vendor.
  • 郛人闻城中之殴缇骑也，亦殴之，焚其舟，挤水中 - The people of the city, hearing about the attack on the guards, also attack the guards, burn their boat, and push them into the water.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日雨霁 - The next day after the rain stopped; '霁' (jì) specifically refers to the cessation of rain.
  • 乡大夫素服谒两台 - '乡大夫' refers to local officials or elders; '素服' means wearing plain or mourning clothes; '谒' means to pay respects or visit; '两台' refers to two high-ranking officials, possibly the provincial governor and the military commander.
  • 策所以敉地方 - '策' means strategy or plan; '敉' (mǐ) means to pacify or govern; '地方' refers to the local area or region.
  • 夜已密书飞骑白东厂 - '夜已密书' means secretly writing a letter at night; '飞骑' means sending a fast horse messenger; '白东厂' means reporting to the eunuch-led secret police (Dongchang).
  • 草疏告变 - '草疏' means drafting a memorial; '告变' means reporting a rebellion or uprising.
  • 檄下县曰 - '檄' is an official document or proclamation; '下县' means sending it to the county level.
  • 谁为柝声聚众者 - '柝声' refers to the sound of a watchman's drum or bell used to gather people; '聚众' means gathering a crowd.
  • 谁为爇香号泣者 - '爇香' means burning incense; '号泣' means crying out in distress.
  • 谁为骁雄贾勇、党罪囚而戕天使者 - '骁雄' means brave and strong individuals; '贾勇' means boasting of courage; '党罪囚' means joining with criminals; '戕天使' means harming an imperial envoy.
  • 必悉诛无赦 - Must be all killed without pardon.
  • 始，众以吏部故，用义气相感发 - '始' means initially; '众' means the crowd; '以吏部故' means because of the Ministry of Personnel; '用义气相感发' means inspired by righteousness.
  • 五人一呼，千百为群 - '五人一呼' means five people calling out; '千百为群' means forming groups of hundreds.
  • 闻捕诛，稍稍惧 - '闻捕诛' means hearing about the capture and execution; '稍稍惧' means gradually becoming afraid.
  • 五人毅然出自承曰 - '毅然' means resolutely; '出自承' means coming forward to confess.
  • 俱就系 - All were arrested.
  • 吾侪小人，从吏部死，死且不朽 - '吾侪小人' means 'we lowly people'; '从吏部死' means to die alongside the Ministry of Personnel; '死且不朽' means to die but remain immortal.
  • 及吏部死诏狱 - '及' means when; '死诏狱' means dying in a special prison for high-ranking officials.
  • 五人亦斩于吴市 - Beheaded in the market of Wu.
  • 谈笑自若 - Talking and laughing as usual.
  • 先刑一日，暴风雨，太湖水溢 - '先刑一日' means the day before the execution; '暴风雨' means a violent storm; '太湖水溢' means the water of Tai Lake overflowing.
  • 广陵人则言文焕家居昼坐，忽忽见五人严装仗剑，旌旆导吏部来，忽不见 - '广陵人' means people from Guangling; '文焕' is a person's name; '家居昼坐' means sitting at home during the day; '忽忽见五人严装仗剑' means suddenly seeing five people dressed in armor with swords; '旌旆导吏部来' means leading the Ministry of Personnel with banners; '忽不见' means suddenly disappearing.
  • 庭井石阑，飞起舞空中，良久乃堕，声轰如雷 - '庭井石阑' means the stone railings around the courtyard well; '飞起舞空中' means flying up and dancing in the air; '良久乃堕' means after a long time falling down; '声轰如雷' means making a loud sound like thunder.
  • 明年，烈皇帝即位，忠贤伏诛，吏部子茂兰刺血上冤状 - '明年' means the next year; '烈皇帝' refers to the Emperor; '即位' means ascending the throne; '忠贤伏诛' means the eunuch Wei Zhongxian being executed; '吏部子茂兰' means the son of the Ministry of Personnel, Maolan; '刺血上冤状' means submitting a petition of injustice with blood.
  • 诏恤吏部，诛文焕 - '诏恤' means issuing an edict to show sympathy; '诛文焕' means executing Wen Huan.

  • 人道之所存 - The essence of humanity.
  • 百年来第一快心事也 - The most satisfying event in a hundred years.
  • 浮一大白 - To drink a large cup of wine to celebrate.
  • 箫非箫也，盖古“尺八” - The flute is not a flute but an ancient 'shicha'.
  • 弄俗箫成声 - To play a common flute and produce sound.
  • 恶其声 - To dislike the sound.
  • 得吴市箫吹之 - To get a flute from Wu and play it.
  • 剡刳刻镂 - To carve and engrave.
  • 大变旧法 - To greatly change the old methods.
  • 操造水滨怪石旁 - To make and play by the water and beside strange stones.
  • 入幽岫林樾苍蒨中 - To enter deep valleys and green woods.
  • 月野霜庭、鸟睡虫醒之际 - At the time when the moon shines on the fields and frost covers the courtyard, birds sleep and insects wake.
  • 启塞抑按 - To open and close, to press and release.
  • 悟其法 - To understand the method.
  • 骂前人聋钝 - To curse the ancients for being deaf and dull.
